visible_agent_inputs.question: What was the name of Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 album released by EMI Records?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Sock-Cess — [3] "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album"
- Sock-Cess — [3] "released exclusively in the UK by EMI"
- The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album) — [2] "released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records."
- The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers — [1] "first released in 1994" and "EMI America record label"

Ambiguities:
- "The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers" is "also titled Greatest Hits on some versions" ([1]) — title variants exist.
- User's word "album" could mean studio album vs. compilation; passage [3] labels Sock-Cess a "compilation album" and a "promotional only release."

Bridge / missing fact:
- The passages provide the needed relation (name of the 1989 EMI-released album) and identify it as "Sock-Cess" ([3]); no additional facts are required to answer the question.

Retrieval direction:
- Open page "Sock-Cess" (Red Hot Chili Peppers album) or search "Sock-Cess Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 EMI"
- Open "Red Hot Chili Peppers discography"
- Open page "Mother's Milk (album)" or search "Mother's Milk 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers" to confirm studio-release distinctions
Final answer (resolved from passages): Sock-Cess.

current_output.query: Sock-Cess Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 EMI

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Agent role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

Pipeline position: after summary1 and before second-hop retrieval. Inputs are the original question and the first-hop intermediate state. Functional responsibility: produce one focused retrieval query that is likely to recover the missing evidence needed by the downstream agents. Query quality is determined by evidence recovery, not by whether the query itself resembles or contains the final answer.

Native output contract: The module must consume `question` and `summary_1` and produce exactly one compact second-hop query field named `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include every distinct entity token visible in the inputs in the single terse query and place at most one neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the targeted relation or attribute (for example: "EntityA EntityB director").
- If a visible disambiguator or attribute token appears adjacent to an entity in the inputs (for example a multiword service name, an explicit page title, a year, or an attribute marker like a birth/producer/service/series cue), include that disambiguator token together with the associated entity in the query rather than dropping or truncating it.
- If `summary_1` contains an explicit page-title token sequence (for example an instruction-like ExactPageTitle or Fetch page title), prefer that exact page-title token sequence as the query value.
- When the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Sock-Cess | Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers and was released exclusively in the UK by EMI as a promotional only release. The album contains tracks from the band's first four studio albums.

The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers | The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers (also titled Greatest Hits on some versions) is a compilation album by the Red Hot Chili Peppers, first released in 1994, containing tracks from the band's time signed to the EMI America record label. " What Hits!? " has all these songs and 8 more. This compilation has been reprinted at least 4 times from 1994 to 2005 with different cover art.

The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album) | The Red Hot Chili Peppers is the debut studio album by American rock band Red Hot Chili Peppers, released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records. The album was produced by Gang of Four guitarist Andy Gill, and is the only album to feature Jack Sherman on guitar. Sherman was fired by the band after the album release tour, replaced by founding member Hillel Slovak. " Get Up and Jump" was the only single released from the album, but a music video was made for the song ...[truncated]

Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers | Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers is a compilation album released on March 31, 1998 by the Red Hot Chili Peppers. All of the songs featured are covers mainly from the band's early albums.
Out in L.A. | Out in L.A. is a compilation of rare tracks by the Red Hot Chili Peppers released November 1, 1994 on EMI.

Ataxia (band) | Ataxia was a short-lived American experimental rock band formed in 2004 by guitarist John Frusciante (Red Hot Chili Peppers), bassist Joe Lally (Fugazi) and drummer Josh Klinghoffer (Red Hot Chili Peppers, Dot Hacker, The Bicycle Thief), who later succeeded Frusciante as the lead guitarist of the Red Hot Chili Peppers.

The Abbey Road E.P. | The Abbey Road E.P. is an EP by The Red Hot Chili Peppers, released in the UK in May 1988 through EMI America as a way of introducing UK fans to the band's back catalog as they were touring the UK that year. Out of the five tracks included on this EP, four had already been previously released on the band's studio albums, although the band's first two albums The Red Hot Chili Peppers and Freaky Styley were not released in the UK until 1990, thus it provided a way of introduc ...[truncated]
